page: Amazon Web Services p3dn.24xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance designed for high-performance computing workloads. It features 96 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8175M processors with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a base frequency of 2.5 GHz. The instance is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For hardware acceleration, it bundles eight NVIDIA Volta V100 GPUs providing a combined 256 GB of VRAM.

Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this instance includes 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and offers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. The storage and network optimization support high-throughput data transfers, which is critical for distributed training and large-scale data ingestion.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance profile. While single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by stress-ng single-core and single-threaded gzip compression tests, multi-core capabilities are highly robust. The instance achieves strong results in multi-core stress-ng tests, PassMark CPU scores, and memory bandwidth tests. Notably, its LLM inference speeds for prompt processing and text generation across small, medium, and large models rank in the top tier or strong categories.

Given the dense integration of eight enterprise-grade GPUs, high memory capacity, and 100 Gbps networking, this instance represents a high-resource tier with corresponding qualitative cost considerations. It is optimized for resource-intensive workloads rather than budget-conscious general computing. The hardware configuration and benchmark performance make it highly suitable for large language model inference, deep learning training, high-performance database operations, and intensive multi-threaded computing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services p3dn.24xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance built on the Nitro hypervisor, featuring 96 vCPUs from Intel Xeon 8175M processors and 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory. It integrates eight NVIDIA Volta V100 GPUs with 256 GB of total VRAM, alongside 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. While single-core benchmarks are modest, the instance delivers strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and top-tier LLM inference speeds for small, medium, and large models. This high-density hardware profile is qualitatively positioned for demanding enterprise workloads. It is best suited for deep learning, large language model inference, high-throughput database operations, and multi-threaded scientific computing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services p3dn.24xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, eight NVIDIA Volta V100 GPUs, 1800 GB NVMe SSD storage, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services p3dn.24xlarge features 96 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, 8 NVIDIA V100 GPUs, and 1800 GB NVMe SSD storage for high-performance workloads.
tagline: A high-density GPU instance combining eight accelerators, massive memory, and high-speed networking for demanding deep learning and inference workloads.
bullet_points: Eight NVIDIA Volta V100 GPUs with 256 GB total VRAM
96 vCPUs via Intel Xeon 8175M processors and 768 GB DDR4 RAM
1800 GB local NVMe SSD storage and 100 Gbps network bandwidth
Top-tier LLM inference performance for small, medium, and large models
Strong multi-core CPU and high-throughput memory bandwidth
Optimized for deep learning, scientific computing, and database operations
